archived_evidence_artifacts.note: no resolution was accepted, so no archived evidence artifact is required; the validator verifies archived GSIS-bearing content (with SHA-256 recomputation) for any future resolved row

3.1_overall_pick_chain.validator_behavior: any 3.1 evidence entry is rejected outright
3.1_overall_pick_chain.udfa_row_note: te-daequan-wright is additionally structurally inapplicable (no overall_pick)

3.2_reviewed_mapping.reason: requires archived GSIS-bearing evidence plus an explicit, attributable named-human sign-off, which has not occurred during this automated implementation
3.2_reviewed_mapping.candidate_note: deliberately none, so no name/team/position-derived candidate could be mistaken for evidence

accounting.unresolved_rows: all 48 locked source identities (enumerated in the markdown report and in the artifact itself)
accounting.unresolved_shared_reason: 3.1 blocked pending second-leg evidence; 3.3 governed artifact does not exist; 3.2 mandatory named-human review has not occurred

identity_coverage_dependency_audit.rationale: no gsis_id was resolved, so no discoverability mechanism can honestly be recorded per row
identity_coverage_dependency_audit.known_source_context: the only identified GSIS-bearing governed source (player_season_coverage_v0.json) is built from accrued NFL statistics; any future resolution sourced from it must record contingent_on_post_draft_participation

validation.tests: npm test passing, including 72 crosswalk tests (positive, all required negative cases, 3.3 exact-key mapping verification, 3.2 corroborating-fact literal verification and independence, mechanically-verified blocked-row disposition with all three reasons individually spoof-tested, identity_coverage_mechanism exact-match verification, immutable-commit-SHA enforcement, inertness scans)

changes: 3.3_governed_artifact evidence now requires the dereferenced archive to contain both the claimed gsis_id and the row's exact source_player_id, plus schema_version agreement where the archive itself declares one
3.2 corroborating facts now have their archived citations independently resolved and hash-verified, and must be materially distinct (no shared archive hash/location/fact text/original_url)

blocked rows now require a machine-verifiable disposition (non-null reviewer/reviewed_at, non-empty notes, at least one resolution_evidence entry) so a bare blocked label can never inflate the decision to ..._complete
the validator now checks the artifact's issue and governing_design fields against the pinned constants, not only source_lock

3.3 evidence now requires exact structured matching: the cited archive is deterministically JSON-parsed (top-level array or a rows array, no substring fallback) and must contain exactly one row whose full four-field governed source key matches this row, with that row's own gsis_id equal to the claim -- zero/multiple/mismatched matches all fail closed, including the specific cross-row string-co-occurrence regression the review required

the CLI's production archived-evidence resolver now reads the exact pinned commit via 'git show <commit>:<path>' against every locally available sibling repo (TIBER-Data, TIBER-Rookies, TIBER-Teamstate, and Forecast itself), fixing the prior mutual incompatibility where the validator required an external 3.3 citation the CLI resolver could never actually read -- honestly scoped as a same-environment fix, not CI-portable until sibling checkouts are provisioned there too
3.2 corroborating facts now require a verified expected_literal actually present in the archived content, not merely an archive that exists and hashes correctly

blocked rows now require a mechanically-verified disqualification_reason (prohibited_method | non_reproducible_or_fabricated_evidence | governed_blocker_citation), each checked against the entry's own content -- a recognized evidence_class token with fabricated reviewer/notes alone (the exact spoof the review demonstrated) is now rejected, tracked via a new verifiedBlockedCount result field and an explicit decision-rule guard

prohibited_method verification now scans only a separate, non-empty attempted_evidence payload for a marker -- never disqualification_reason, disqualification_detail, notes, or reviewer, closing the self-authored-prose loophole where writing the word 'fuzzy' into free text alone verified a block

non_reproducible_or_fabricated_evidence now requires positive proof: the cited archive must actually reproduce, plus a claimed_value the entry asserts was bound, with verification confirming the reproduced content does NOT contain it -- a bare resolver null (equally consistent with an unknown repo, unfetched commit, path typo, or missing sibling checkout in CI) no longer counts as fabrication proof

governed_blocker_citation now requires the same exact-key-match discipline as 3.3: the cited archive is deterministically parsed for a rows array, filtered to the row matching this identity's full four-field governed key (zero/multiple matches fail closed), and that row must itself carry non-empty blocker_reason/blocker_detail fields -- an unrelated-but-reproducible file can no longer pass

identity_coverage_mechanism.citation is now resolved, hash-verified, and exact-key-matched via the same shared parser used by 3.3 and governed_blocker_citation, with the matched row required to itself declare independent_of_post_draft_outcome: true -- previously only citation shape was checked, so a fabricated citation could mark a row independent outright

isValidCitation now requires an immutable full 40-character lowercase-hex git commit SHA everywhere it is used -- a mutable ref (main, HEAD), a branch/tag name, or an abbreviated SHA now fails citation validation

decision_rationale: the governed Lane A artifact, fail-closed validator, tests, and full 48-row accounting are complete and passing, but all 48 identities remain unresolved: §3.2 cannot produce a resolved row without archived GSIS-bearing evidence and an explicit named-human sign-off (which has not occurred), and no governed §3.1/§3.3 evidence exists yet
